history.content:
<thought>
I’m facing upward. The cell directly ahead is a lava cell, so moving forward would be fatal. The only safe immediate move is to turn right, so I can start navigating around the lava barrier.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is safe `.`. Moving forward keeps me in the navigable area and toward the unexplored region, while turning would not advance me toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing upward. The cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. I need to turn to find a safe open path; the left side appears to have open floor nearby, while the right side is also blocked by a wall. Turning left should orient me toward a safer direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07, column C, facing up. The cell directly ahead (row 06, column C) is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. The safe open cell to my right is at row 07, column D, so I should turn right to face it before moving.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. To make progress, I need to reorient. Turning right will let me face toward the open area on my left side of the map from this view, which is the safest available direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D, facing up. The cell directly ahead is safe floor, while the right side is blocked by a wall and the goal is not yet visible. Moving forward is the best way to continue exploring toward the open area and possible goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, and the cell directly in front of me is safe floor. The nearby visible area does not show the goal yet, so the best move is to continue exploring forward rather than turning.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D, facing upward. The cell directly ahead is safe floor, while the right side is blocked by a wall and the goal is not yet visible. Moving forward will let me explore upward toward the unknown area.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing upward. The cell directly ahead is safe floor, while the cells to my left are blocked by a wall at row 06. Since the goal is not visible yet, moving forward is the safest way to explore toward open space.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D, facing up. The cell directly ahead is open floor, so I can safely move forward and continue exploring toward the unknown area and possible goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward. The cell directly ahead is safe floor, while the right side is blocked by a wall and the goal is not yet visible. Moving forward keeps me exploring the open corridor and is the safest progress.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, and the cell directly ahead is unknown. The right side is blocked by a wall one step away, while the left side appears open. To safely explore toward the open area, I will turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. I need to reorient to find a safe path through the open cells on the left side of the map. Turning left will face me toward the open area instead of the blocked wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward. The cell directly ahead appears to be a safe floor cell, while the left side is blocked by a wall and the right side is also open but not immediately needed. Since I should progress through safe cells and avoid lava, moving forward is the best next step.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
